[html]identity.downloads.desc: Encrypted messaging, multi-chain wallet, and decentralized identity — one app, three platforms.
[html]identity.enables.card1Desc: Send and receive end-to-end encrypted messages addressed by your DNA name. No phone number or email required — contacts find you by name alone.
[html]identity.enables.card2Desc: Your seed phrase derives wallets for Cellframe, Ethereum, Solana, and TRON. Send tokens in-chat — recipients are resolved by DNA name, not raw addresses.
[html]identity.enables.card3Desc: Publish to public channels and your personal wall. Every post is signed with your identity key — verifiable authorship without centralized moderation.
[html]identity.enables.card4Desc: One name across every DNA service — present and future. Your identity works as a universal namespace for the entire decentralized ecosystem.
[html]identity.enables.desc: One DNA name unlocks every service in the ecosystem. No separate accounts, no password resets, no email verification — just your key pair.
[html]identity.hero.subtitle:
Ahuman-readable nametied to your cryptographic key pair —
                no phone number, no email, no central authority. One seed generatesall your walletsand one identity you fully own.
[html]identity.how.desc: Registration happens directly insideDNA Connect— the app is your wallet. Three steps, no web forms, no fees.
[html]identity.how.step1: Create or restoreyour wallet in DNA Connect from a single seed phrase.
[html]identity.how.step2: Choose a unique DNA name(3–20 characters). The name is checked for uniqueness and bound to your public key on the Nodus DHT network.
[html]identity.how.step3: Use it everywhere— message contacts, manage wallets, vote on proposals, and interact across all CPUNK services with one identity.
[html]identity.how.title: HowDNA Identity Works
[html]identity.pillars.card2Desc: A single seed generates your DNA name and all wallets — CF20, ETH, SOL, TRC. One identity across every chain, no duplicates.
[html]identity.pillars.desc: DNA (Decentralized Network Applications) replaces centralized logins with a self-sovereign identity layer. Ownership is defined by your private key — not by a company or service provider.
[html]identity.security.bullet1: Pseudonymous by default— DNA names need not reveal real-world identity
[html]identity.security.bullet2: No phone or emailrequired to register or use your identity
[html]identity.security.bullet3: Immutable recordsstored across the distributed Nodus network
[html]identity.security.bullet4: You controlwhich metadata fields are exposed — nothing more
[html]identity.security.bullet5: No single point of failure— fully decentralized storage and resolution
[html]identity.security.bullet6: Mathematically verifiableownership proofs via post-quantum signatures
[html]identity.security.title: Security &Privacy
[html]identity.tech.card1Desc: Every identity operation — registration, updates, authentication — is signed withML-DSA-87(NIST FIPS 204). 2,592-byte public keys provide NIST Category 5 security.
[html]identity.tech.card2Desc: Identity records containing sensitive data are encrypted withML-KEM-1024(NIST FIPS 203). Key encapsulation ensures only the intended recipient can decrypt.
[html]identity.tech.card3Desc: Identity records are stored across theNodus DHT network— replicated on multiple nodes for redundancy. No single server holds your identity.
[html]prod_connect.arch.desc: DNA Connect runs onNodus— a post-quantum Kademlia DHT with ML-DSA + ML-KEM support, PBFT consensus, and a 6-node production cluster.
[html]prod_connect.features.channels_des: Open chat rooms where people hang out and talk — like Telegram groups, but decentralized. Every message is signed withDilithium5so you always know who said what.
[html]prod_connect.features.dm_desc: Private conversations encrypted withKyber1024 + AES-256-GCM. Address contacts by their DNA name — no phone numbers or email needed.
[html]prod_connect.features.groups_desc: Decentralized groups withGEK encryption— efficient key distribution for groups of any size. Automatic key rotation on member changes.
[html]prod_connect.feed.title: Your timelineWall posts, images, and reactions
[html]prod_connect.hero.lead:
Afully decentralized messengerwithNIST Category 5 post-quantum encryption.
              No central servers, no phone numbers, no metadata collection. Messages travel through a peer-to-peer network —
              encrypted end-to-end withKyber1024 + AES-256-GCM.
[html]prod_connect.hero.title: Post-QuantumEncrypted Messenger
[html]prod_connect.wallet.li1: Cellframe, Ethereum, Solana, TRON— one wallet, four chains, native signing per chain
[html]prod_connect.wallet.li2: In-chat transfers— send tokens to any DNA contact directly
[html]prod_connect.wallet.li3: DEX swaps— best price from multiple exchanges (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Jupiter, Cellframe DEX)
[html]prod_connect.wallet.title: Multi-Chain WalletBuilt into the messenger

[html]prod_identity.how.step3: Use it everywhere— message contacts, manage wallets, vote on proposals, and interact across all CPUNK services with one identity.

community.decentralization.desc: No central authority, no single point of failure. The network is owned and operated by its community.
community.decentralization.title: Decentralization
community.desc: CPUNK is not a company — it's a movement. Everything is built, governed, and owned by the community.
community.governance.desc: Propose and vote on ecosystem changes through CIPs. Every voice counts, every vote matters.

community.label: The Community
community.privacy.desc: Your data belongs to you. No tracking, no surveillance, no compromise. Privacy is a right, not a feature.
community.privacy.title: Privacy
community.quantum.desc: Built on post-quantum cryptography. Secured today against tomorrow's quantum computers.
community.quantum.title: Quantum Safety
community.title: By the people, for the people
cta.text: Download DNA Connect, join the conversation, and find a piece of the project you want to improve. Every meaningful contributor once started as a community member.

dna.desc: DNA Connect combines post-quantum messaging, a multi-chain wallet, and decentralized identity into a single client — no phone numbers, no central servers, no compromises.
dna.identity.desc: Your DNA name is your identity — tied to a cryptographic key pair, not to a phone number or email. Register once and be reachable across the entire network.
dna.identity.title: Decentralized Identity
dna.label: DNA Connect
dna.messaging.desc: Post-quantum encrypted conversations over a fully decentralized network. No central servers, no metadata. Offline messages wait in distributed mailboxes until you come back online.

dna.title: Three utilities, one app

identity.pillars.card1Desc: Your identity is a cryptographic key pair. No company can revoke, reassign, or censor it. Only your private key defines ownership.
identity.pillars.card3Desc: Protected by NIST Category 5 lattice-based cryptography (ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87). Secure today and against future quantum computers.
identity.pillars.card4Desc: One DNA name across messaging, wallet, governance, and all CPUNK services. It acts as your universal namespace within the ecosystem.

identity.pillars.title: Identity built for the post-quantum era
identity.security.desc: DNA is designed to survive the transition to quantum computing while collecting the absolute minimum amount of data.
identity.tech.card1Spec1: 2,592 B public key · 4,896 B secret key
identity.tech.card2Spec1: 1,568 B public key · 3,168 B secret key
identity.tech.desc: DNA Identity is built on NIST-standardized post-quantum cryptography. Every identity record is a structured, signed document stored across the Nodus DHT network.
identity.tech.label: Technology
identity.tech.title: Under the hood

messenger.features.channels.desc: Open chat rooms where people hang out and talk — like Telegram groups, but decentralized. Every message is signed withDilithium5so you always know who said what.
messenger.features.channels.title: Public Channels
messenger.features.desc: Encrypted direct messages, Telegram-style public channels, personal wall posts, and the ability to send funds directly in conversation — all running on a decentralized network with zero central servers.
messenger.features.dm.desc: Private conversations encrypted withKyber1024 + AES-256-GCM. Address contacts by their DNA name — no phone numbers or email needed.
messenger.features.dm.title: Encrypted Direct Messages
messenger.features.groups.desc: Decentralized groups withGEK encryption— efficient key distribution for groups of any size. Automatic key rotation on member changes.
messenger.features.groups.title: Group Messaging
messenger.features.label: Features
messenger.features.title: Private messaging. Public channels. Send funds in chat.
messenger.features.wall.desc: A personal public feed tied to your DNA identity. Share updates, thoughts, or announcements that anyone can see on your profile.
messenger.features.wall.title: Wall Posts
messenger.hero.lead:
Afully decentralized messengerwithNIST Category 5 post-quantum encryption.
              No central servers, no phone numbers, no metadata collection. Messages travel through a peer-to-peer network —
              encrypted end-to-end withKyber1024 + AES-256-GCM.
messenger.hero.title: Post-QuantumEncrypted Messenger
messenger.security.desc: 256-bit security level. Protected against both current classical attacks and future quantum computers. Your keys never leave your device.
messenger.security.label: Security
messenger.security.pillar1.desc: Messages travel peer-to-peer through the DHT. Nothing to compromise or subpoena.
messenger.security.pillar1.title: No Central Servers
messenger.security.pillar2.desc: Your real IP is never exposed to contacts or third parties. DHT-only transport.
messenger.security.pillar2.title: No IP Leakage
messenger.security.pillar3.desc: Minimal routing header. Cannot track who talks to whom.
messenger.security.pillar3.title: No Metadata
messenger.security.pillar4.desc: Identity via DNA name tied to your cryptographic key pair. No personal data required.
messenger.security.pillar4.title: No Phone Number
messenger.security.title: NIST Category 5 — maximum quantum resistance
messenger.showcase.desc: The home feed shows wall posts from people you follow. Share images, react, reply, repost — a decentralized social layer built on top of the encrypted network.
messenger.showcase.label: Home Feed
messenger.showcase.li1: Image posts with full media support
messenger.showcase.li3: All content signed and stored on the DHT
messenger.showcase.title: Your timelineWall posts, images, and reactions
messenger.tech.desc: All cryptography, messaging, and DHT operations run in a native C library (libdna). Flutter, Android, and CLI connect through thin FFI bindings — zero crypto in managed code.
messenger.tech.label: Technology
messenger.tech.pillar1.desc: Async task queue with domain modules: messaging, identity, contacts, DHT, backup. Thread pool for concurrent execution.
messenger.tech.pillar1.title: Modular Engine
messenger.tech.pillar2.desc: Same C code compiles for Linux, Windows, and Android. Flutter via dart:ffi, Android via JNI, CLI for automation.
messenger.tech.pillar2.title: Cross-Platform
messenger.tech.pillar3.desc: C library, Nodus server, and shared crypto are Apache 2.0 licensed. DNA Connect app is source-available for auditing.
messenger.tech.pillar3.title: Open Source
messenger.tech.pillar4.desc: ML-KEM-1024 key exchange, ML-DSA-87 signatures, SHA3-512 hashing, AES-256-GCM encryption. Maximum quantum resistance.
messenger.tech.pillar4.title: NIST Category 5
messenger.tech.title: Post-quantum C engine under the hood
nodus_page.arch.desc: The Nodus network operates using a two-tier architecture designed to separate core network operations from client access.
nodus_page.arch.label: Architecture
nodus_page.arch.tier1_desc1: The node backbone of the network. Nodus nodes communicate directly with each other usingTCPandUDP.
nodus_page.arch.tier1_desc2: This layer maintains the distributed hash table, performs peer discovery, and handles message routing across the global network. Tier 1 nodes form the core mesh infrastructure that keeps the network decentralized and resilient.
nodus_page.arch.tier2_desc1: The client access layer. Applications such as DNA Connect connect to Nodus nodes overTCP.
nodus_page.arch.tier2_desc2: Through this connection, clients can resolve DNA identities, send encrypted messages, retrieve offline messages, publish or fetch distributed data, and communicate with other peers through the network. Clients do not communicate directly with each other — they interact with the Nodus network through connected nodes.
nodus_page.arch.title: Two-Tier Architecture
nodus_page.cta.text:
Download DNA Connect, join the conversation, and find a piece of the project
              you want to improve. Every meaningful contributor once started as a community member.
nodus_page.cta.title: Join the movement
nodus_page.design.desc1: Nodus removes the need for centralized messaging servers. Instead of relying on a single infrastructure provider, the network operates as a global peer-to-peer mesh where nodes collectively maintain routing, discovery, and temporary storage.
nodus_page.design.desc3: The network continues to function as long as nodes remain connected.
nodus_page.design.li1: Resilience— no single point of failure
nodus_page.design.li2: Censorship resistance— no central authority to block
nodus_page.design.li3: Availability— the network is always on
nodus_page.design.li4: Privacy— nodes only see encrypted blobs
nodus_page.design.title: Decentralizedby Design
nodus_page.dht.desc1: At its core, Nodus operates as a distributed hash table. The DHT allows nodes to locate information and peers without centralized coordination.
nodus_page.dht.desc2: Each node is responsible for a portion of the keyspace and can store or route data associated with those keys. This mechanism allows the network to:
nodus_page.dht.desc3: All operations are performed collectively by the network nodes.
nodus_page.dht.title: DistributedHash Table
nodus_page.downloads.desc: Encrypted messaging, multi-chain wallet, and decentralized identity — one app, three platforms.
nodus_page.downloads.label: Downloads
nodus_page.downloads.title: Get DNA Connect
nodus_page.ecosystem.connect_desc: Post-quantum encrypted communication — messages routed and delivered through the Nodus network with offline support.
nodus_page.ecosystem.desc: Nodus is the infrastructure layer of the DNA ecosystem. While DNA applications provide user-facing functionality, Nodus provides the underlying decentralized networking system that connects identities and peers.
nodus_page.ecosystem.future_desc: Any decentralized application can build on Nodus — forming a post-quantum capable decentralized communication stack.
nodus_page.ecosystem.identity_desc: Decentralized identity resolution — human-readable names tied to cryptographic key pairs, resolved through the Nodus DHT.
nodus_page.ecosystem.label: Ecosystem
nodus_page.ecosystem.title: Role in the DNA Ecosystem
nodus_page.hero.meta1: No central servers. No single point of failure.
nodus_page.hero.subtitle1:
The Nodus Network is thedecentralized networking layerof the DNA protocol.
                It provides peer discovery, routing, identity resolution, and distributed storage without relying on centralized servers.
nodus_page.hero.subtitle2: Nodus is built as a post-quantum secure distributed hash table (DHT) network where nodes cooperate to locate peers, route encrypted messages, and store temporary data across the network. All operations are cryptographically signed (ML-DSA-87), with built-in support for key encapsulation (ML-KEM-1024) and AES-256-GCM encryption.
nodus_page.hero.subtitle3: Instead of relying on a central infrastructure, the Nodus network forms a self-organizing mesh of nodes that collectively maintain the routing and discovery layer used by DNA services such as DNA Connect and DNA Identity.
nodus_page.hero.title: DNANodus
nodus_page.identity.desc1: DNA identities are resolved through the Nodus network. When a client looks up a DNA name, the request is routed through the DHT until it reaches the nodes responsible for that identity's record — no centralized registries or servers involved.
nodus_page.identity.desc2: Each identity record contains the data needed to establish secure communication:
nodus_page.identity.li1: Dilithium5 public key— verifies signatures and proves ownership
nodus_page.identity.li2: Kyber1024 public key— enables post-quantum key encapsulation
nodus_page.identity.li3: Wallet addresses— Cellframe, Ethereum, Solana, TRON
nodus_page.identity.li4: Presence status— online, offline, or last-seen timestamp
nodus_page.identity.title: IdentityResolution
nodus_page.offline.desc1: The Nodus network supports offline message delivery. When a recipient is offline, encrypted messages are temporarily stored across distributed storage nodes until the recipient reconnects and retrieves them.
nodus_page.offline.desc2: This enables asynchronous communication while maintaining full decentralization — no central inbox server, no metadata leakage.
nodus_page.offline.li1: 7-day TTL— messages persist on the network for up to 7 days
nodus_page.offline.li2: End-to-end encrypted— storage nodes see only encrypted blobs
nodus_page.offline.li3: Automatic retrieval— clients pull pending messages on reconnect
nodus_page.offline.li4: Multi-node replication— messages stored on k closest nodes for redundancy
nodus_page.offline.title: OfflineMessage Delivery
nodus_page.specs.desc: Nodus is a pure C implementation with no C++ dependencies. Every protocol operation is cryptographically signed and verified.
nodus_page.specs.encryption_purpose: Post-quantum key exchange and payload encryption
nodus_page.specs.keyspace_purpose: DHT address space for node and data routing
nodus_page.specs.label: Specifications
nodus_page.specs.opensource_desc: Pure C codebase under Apache 2.0. No external crypto dependencies — all post-quantum primitives are implemented in the shared crypto layer.
nodus_page.specs.replication_purpose: Data stored on 8 closest nodes for redundancy
nodus_page.specs.signing_purpose: All protocol operations signed and verified
nodus_page.specs.tier1_desc: Kademlia peer discovery: ping, find_node, put, get. Lightweight node-to-node protocol for maintaining the distributed routing table.
nodus_page.specs.tier2_desc: Client access layer: authentication, DHT operations, listen/subscribe, channels, and witness BFT. Full-duplex framed connections.
nodus_page.specs.title: Technical specifications
nodus_page.specs.ttl_purpose: Temporary storage lifetime for messages and records
nodus_page.specs.wire_purpose: Compact binary encoding over framed TCP/UDP
privacy.effective_date: Effective Date: March 17, 2026 · Last Updated: April 5, 2026
privacy.s1.highlight:
DNA Connect does not collect, store, or transmit any personal data to centralized servers.
                All communication is end-to-end encrypted using NIST-approved post-quantum algorithms
                (Kyber1024 for key encapsulation, Dilithium5 for signatures).
privacy.s1.p1:
DNA Connect is a decentralized, peer-to-peer messaging application built on post-quantum cryptography.
              This privacy policy explains what data the application accesses, how it is used, and your rights as a user.
privacy.s1.title: 1. Overview
privacy.s10.p1:
We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Any changes will be reflected on this page
              with an updated "Last Updated" date. Continued use of DNA Connect after changes constitutes
              acceptance of the revised policy.
privacy.s10.title: 10. Changes to This Policy
privacy.s11.p1: If you have questions about this privacy policy or DNA Connect's data practices:
privacy.s11.title: 11. Contact
privacy.s2.li2: Message content — all messages are end-to-end encrypted and only readable by the intended recipient
privacy.s2.li3: Contact lists or address books from your device
privacy.s2.li8: Browsing history or app usage patterns
privacy.s2.p2: One exception — opt-in debug logs. The app offers a feature at Settings → Data & Storage → Send Debug Log to Developer that lets you voluntarily send your application log to the developer for troubleshooting. When you use this feature: the log is scrubbed of mnemonics, private keys, and credential-like values; then hybrid-encrypted (Kyber1024 + AES-256-GCM) to the developer's public key; and delivered via the DHT. No other party (including DHT nodes) can read the log. This feature is entirely optional and only runs when you explicitly tap the button. Logs expire after 1 hour if not delivered.
privacy.s2.p1: DNA Connect is designed with privacy as its core principle. We do not collect:
privacy.s2.title: 2. Data We Do NOT Collect
privacy.s3.app_r3_purpose: Caching display names, avatars, and public keys of contacts
privacy.s3.app_r4_purpose: Offline access to public posts, comments, and likes
privacy.s3.app_r5_purpose: Group memberships, encrypted group keys, channel subscriptions
privacy.s3.app_r6_purpose: Displaying wallet balances and transaction history
privacy.s3.app_r6_storage: Local SQLite database (wallet_cache.db). Private keys are never stored — derived on-demand from recovery phrase.
privacy.s3.app_r8_storage: PBKDF2-SHA256 hash with random salt, stored in Android Keystore / iOS Keychain (encrypted by OS)
privacy.s3.app_r9_purpose: Settings such as biometric lock, notification preferences
privacy.s3.crypto_r1_storage: Local file (identity.dsa), derived from recovery phrase. On Android, wrapped by Android Keystore TEE (hardware-backed AES-256-GCM) — the wrapping key never leaves secure hardware.
privacy.s3.crypto_r2_storage: Local file (identity.kem), derived from recovery phrase. On Android, wrapped by Android Keystore TEE (hardware-backed AES-256-GCM) — the wrapping key never leaves secure hardware.
privacy.s3.crypto_r3_storage: Encrypted with Kyber1024 public key (mnemonic.enc). Never stored as plain text.
privacy.s3.crypto_r4_storage: Cached locally (dht_identity.bin), deterministically derived from recovery phrase
privacy.s3.p1: The following data is stored exclusively on your device and never transmitted to us:
privacy.s3.p2: Local SQLite databases are encrypted at rest using SQLCipher (AES-256-CBC with per-page HMAC-SHA512 integrity). Each database's encryption key is derived from your identity material using PBKDF2 and stored in the OS keystore (Android Keystore / iOS Keychain). If your device is lost or stolen, database contents cannot be read even with physical access. Sensitive cryptographic material (recovery phrase, PIN) uses additional dedicated encryption as described above.
privacy.s3.title: 3. Data Stored Locally on Your Device
privacy.s4.encrypted_li1:
Direct messages:Encrypted with Kyber1024 key encapsulation and AES-256-GCM.
                Only the intended recipient can decrypt them.
privacy.s4.encrypted_li2:
Group messages:Encrypted with a Group Encryption Key (GEK), distributed
                to group members via Kyber1024 encapsulation.
privacy.s4.encrypted_li3: Contact lists and address books:Self-encrypted with your own Kyber1024 public key.
privacy.s4.encrypted_li4: Message backups:Self-encrypted with your own Kyber1024 public key.
privacy.s4.encrypted_li5: Group membership lists:Self-encrypted with your own Kyber1024 public key.
privacy.s4.encrypted_li6: Media attachments (images, videos, voice messages):Encrypted with Kyber1024 key encapsulation + AES-256-GCM. Files are chunked and stored on the Nodus media storage layer; only the intended recipient (or group members) can decrypt them.
privacy.s4.encrypted_title: Encrypted Data (not readable by DHT nodes)
privacy.s4.p1:
DNA Connect uses a decentralized hash table (DHT) network called Nodus for message delivery and
              profile discovery. Data published to this network falls into two categories:
privacy.s4.p2:
No centralized server stores or has access to your private keys.
              DHT nodes can read public profile information and wall posts, but
              cannot read your private messages, contact lists, or group memberships.
privacy.s4.public_li1:
Public profile:Your public cryptographic keys, registered display name, bio,
                avatar, location, website, social links, and wallet addresses. This data is intentionally
                public to allow others to find and communicate with you.
privacy.s4.public_li2:
Wall posts, comments, and likes:Public posts you choose to publish, along with
                any comments or likes, are visible to anyone on the network. Posts are signed but not encrypted.
privacy.s4.public_li3:
Channel posts and metadata:Public channel content including channel name,
                description, and posts are readable by DHT nodes.
privacy.s4.public_title: Public Data (readable by DHT nodes and other users)
privacy.s4.title: 4. Decentralized Network (DHT)
privacy.s5.p1: DNA Connect requests the following Android permissions:
privacy.s5.p2:
No permission is used for advertising, tracking, or data collection.
              Camera and biometric access are only activated when you explicitly use those features.
privacy.s5.perm_r1_purpose: Connect to the decentralized DHT network for messaging
privacy.s5.perm_r2_purpose: Detect online/offline status for message delivery
privacy.s5.perm_r3_purpose: Take photos for profile avatar and scan QR codes for adding contacts
privacy.s5.perm_r4_purpose: App lock feature — authenticate with fingerprint or face recognition
privacy.s5.perm_r7_purpose: Detect Wi-Fi connectivity for optimizing network connections
privacy.s5.perm_r8_purpose: Keep background message delivery active (user-triggered from Settings)
privacy.s5.title: 5. Device Permissions
privacy.s6.li1: No Google Analytics or Firebase Analytics
privacy.s6.li4: No crash reporting services that transmit data externally
privacy.s6.li5:
Blockchain RPC endpoints(Cellframe, Ethereum, Solana, TRON) for wallet balance
                queries and transaction broadcasting. These connections transmit only your public wallet address —
                never private keys.
privacy.s6.li6:
BitcoinTry API(api.bitcointry.com) for real-time cryptocurrency price data.
                No personal data or wallet addresses are sent — only token pair identifiers (e.g., ETH_USDT).
privacy.s6.p1: DNA Connect does not integrate any third-party analytics, advertising, or tracking services. Specifically:
privacy.s6.p2: The application connects to the following third-party services for wallet functionality:
privacy.s6.title: 6. Third-Party Services
privacy.s7.p1: All cryptographic operations use NIST-approved post-quantum algorithms:
privacy.s7.p2:
These algorithms are designed to resist attacks from both classical and quantum computers.
              Your private keys never leave your device.
privacy.s7.title: 7. Encryption & Security
privacy.s8.li1: All data is stored on your device. Uninstalling the app deletes all local data.
privacy.s8.li2:
Public profile data on the DHT network is stored permanently to ensure your contacts can always find you.
                Your device periodically refreshes this data to keep it current.
privacy.s8.li3: Wall posts have a 30-day TTL on the network and expire automatically.
privacy.s8.li4: You can delete your account at any time by uninstalling the app. There is no server-side account to delete.
privacy.s8.title: 8. Data Retention & Deletion
privacy.s9.p1:
DNA Connect does not knowingly collect any information from children under the age of 13.
              The application does not require any personal information to create an account —
              only a locally-generated cryptographic key pair.
privacy.s9.title: 9. Children's Privacy
privacy.subtitle: DNA Connect — Post-Quantum Encrypted Messenger
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
prod_connect.arch.label: Architecture
prod_connect.arch.title: Serverless peer-to-peer stack
prod_connect.cta.text: Download DNA Connect, join the conversation, and help build the future of private communication.
prod_connect.cta.title: Join the movement
prod_connect.downloads.desc: Open beta — available for Android, Linux, and Windows. Open source under GPLv3.
prod_connect.downloads.label: Downloads
prod_connect.downloads.title: Get DNA Connect
prod_connect.features.desc: Encrypted direct messages, Telegram-style public channels, personal wall posts, and a built-in multi-chain wallet — all running on a decentralized network with zero central servers.
prod_connect.features.label: Features
prod_connect.features.title: Private messaging. Public channels. Your wallet.
prod_connect.features.wall_desc: A personal public feed tied to your DNA identity. Share updates, thoughts, or announcements that anyone can see on your profile.
prod_connect.feed.desc: The home feed shows wall posts from people you follow. Share images, react, reply, repost — a decentralized social layer built on top of the encrypted network.
prod_connect.feed.label: Home Feed
prod_connect.feed.li1: Image posts with full media support
prod_connect.feed.li3: All content signed and stored on the DHT
prod_connect.security.desc: 256-bit security level. Protected against both current classical attacks and future quantum computers. Your keys never leave your device.
prod_connect.security.label: Security
prod_connect.security.pillar1_desc: Messages travel peer-to-peer through the DHT. Nothing to compromise or subpoena.
prod_connect.security.pillar2_desc: Your real IP is never exposed to contacts or third parties. DHT-only transport.
prod_connect.security.pillar3_desc: Minimal routing header. Cannot track who talks to whom.
prod_connect.security.pillar4_desc: Identity via DNA name tied to your cryptographic key pair. No personal data required.
prod_connect.security.title: NIST Category 5 — maximum quantum resistance
prod_connect.tech.desc: All cryptography, messaging, and DHT operations run in a native C library (libdna). Flutter, Android, and CLI connect through thin FFI bindings — zero crypto in managed code.
prod_connect.tech.label: Technology
prod_connect.tech.pillar1_desc: Async task queue with domain modules: messaging, identity, wallet, contacts, DHT, backup. Thread pool for concurrent execution.
prod_connect.tech.pillar2_desc: Same C code compiles for Linux, Windows, and Android. Flutter via dart:ffi, Android via JNI, CLI for automation.
prod_connect.tech.pillar3_desc: C library, Nodus server, and shared crypto are Apache 2.0 licensed. DNA Connect app is source-available for auditing.
prod_connect.tech.pillar4_desc: ML-KEM-1024 key exchange, ML-DSA-87 signatures, SHA3-512 hashing, AES-256-GCM encryption. Maximum quantum resistance.
prod_connect.tech.title: Post-quantum C engine under the hood
prod_connect.wallet.desc: A single recovery phrase derives wallets across four blockchains. Send and receive tokens without leaving the conversation.
prod_connect.wallet.label: Wallet
prod_identity.cta.text:
Download DNA Connect, join the conversation, and find a piece of the project
              you want to improve. Every meaningful contributor once started as a community member.
prod_identity.cta.title: Join the movement
prod_identity.hero.meta1: Self-sovereign identity for the post-quantum era.
prod_identity.pillars.card1_desc: Your identity is a cryptographic key pair. No company can revoke, reassign, or censor it. Only your private key defines ownership.
prod_identity.pillars.card2_desc: A single seed generates your DNA name and all wallets — CF20, ETH, SOL, TRC. One identity across every chain, no duplicates.
prod_identity.pillars.card3_desc: Protected by NIST Category 5 lattice-based cryptography (ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87). Secure today and against future quantum computers.
prod_identity.pillars.card4_desc: One DNA name across messaging, wallet, governance, and all CPUNK services. It acts as your universal namespace within the ecosystem.
prod_identity.pillars.desc: DNA (Decentralized Network Applications) replaces centralized logins with a self-sovereign identity layer. Ownership is defined by your private key — not by a company or service provider.
prod_identity.pillars.label: Core Pillars
prod_identity.pillars.title: Identity built for the post-quantum era
prod_identity.security.desc: DNA is designed to survive the transition to quantum computing while collecting the absolute minimum amount of data.
prod_nodus.arch.desc: The Nodus network operates using a two-tier architecture designed to separate core network operations from client access.
prod_nodus.arch.label: Architecture
prod_nodus.arch.tier1_desc2: This layer maintains the distributed hash table, performs peer discovery, and handles message routing across the global network. Tier 1 nodes form the core mesh infrastructure that keeps the network decentralized and resilient.
prod_nodus.arch.tier2_desc2: Through this connection, clients can resolve DNA identities, send encrypted messages, retrieve offline messages, publish or fetch distributed data, and communicate with other peers through the network. Clients do not communicate directly with each other — they interact with the Nodus network through connected nodes.
prod_nodus.arch.title: Two-Tier Architecture
prod_nodus.cta.text:
Download DNA Connect, join the conversation, and find a piece of the project
              you want to improve. Every meaningful contributor once started as a community member.
prod_nodus.cta.title: Join the movement
prod_nodus.design.desc1: Nodus removes the need for centralized messaging servers. Instead of relying on a single infrastructure provider, the network operates as a global peer-to-peer mesh where nodes collectively maintain routing, discovery, and temporary storage.
prod_nodus.design.desc3: The network continues to function as long as nodes remain connected.
prod_nodus.dht.desc1: At its core, Nodus operates as a distributed hash table. The DHT allows nodes to locate information and peers without centralized coordination.
prod_nodus.dht.desc2: Each node is responsible for a portion of the keyspace and can store or route data associated with those keys. This mechanism allows the network to:
prod_nodus.dht.desc3: All operations are performed collectively by the network nodes.
prod_nodus.ecosystem.card1_desc: Decentralized identity resolution — human-readable names tied to cryptographic key pairs, resolved through the Nodus DHT.
prod_nodus.ecosystem.card2_desc: Post-quantum encrypted communication — messages routed and delivered through the Nodus network with offline support.
prod_nodus.ecosystem.card3_desc: Any decentralized application can build on Nodus — forming a post-quantum capable decentralized communication stack.
prod_nodus.ecosystem.desc: Nodus is the infrastructure layer of the DNA ecosystem. While DNA applications provide user-facing functionality, Nodus provides the underlying decentralized networking system that connects identities and peers.
prod_nodus.ecosystem.label: Ecosystem
prod_nodus.ecosystem.title: Role in the DNA Ecosystem
prod_nodus.hero.meta1: No central servers. No single point of failure.
prod_nodus.hero.subtitle2: Nodus is built as a post-quantum secure distributed hash table (DHT) network where nodes cooperate to locate peers, route encrypted messages, and store temporary data across the network. All operations are cryptographically signed (ML-DSA-87), with built-in support for key encapsulation (ML-KEM-1024) and AES-256-GCM encryption.
prod_nodus.hero.subtitle3: Instead of relying on a central infrastructure, the Nodus network forms a self-organizing mesh of nodes that collectively maintain the routing and discovery layer used by DNA services such as DNA Connect and DNA Identity.
prod_nodus.identity.desc1: DNA identities are resolved through the Nodus network.
prod_nodus.identity.desc2: When a client attempts to locate a DNA identity, the request is routed through the DHT until it reaches the nodes responsible for that identity's record.
prod_nodus.identity.desc3: This process allows identity lookups to occur without centralized registries or servers.
prod_nodus.offline.desc1: The Nodus network supports offline message delivery.
prod_nodus.offline.desc2: When a recipient is offline, encrypted messages can be temporarily stored within the network using distributed storage nodes.
prod_nodus.offline.desc3: When the recipient reconnects, their client retrieves the stored messages from the network.
prod_nodus.offline.desc4: This allows asynchronous communication while maintaining decentralization.
team.contribute.build_desc:
Contribute to open-source repositories, implement features,
                harden security, and maintain DNA Connect and supporting services.
team.contribute.desc:
CPUNK moves forward when the community does. Pick the area that matches your skills
              and start making an impact.
team.contribute.design_desc:
Work on UI/UX for apps and websites, create visuals, animations,
                infographics, and branding that explain CPUNK to new users.
team.contribute.explain_desc:
Write documentation, tutorials, blog posts, and translations.
                Help others understand how DNA identity and post-quantum cryptography work together.
team.contribute.grow_desc:
Share updates on social media, invite new contributors, organize
                calls or meetups, and connect CPUNK with the broader Web3 ecosystem.
team.contribute.label: Contribute
team.contribute.roadmap_desc:
Participate in discussions, proposals, and governance decisions.
                Help align development priorities with long-term protocol goals.
team.contribute.title: Your skills, your impact
team.contribute.validators_desc:
Operate nodes and validators that process transactions,
                validate DNA registrations, and participate in consensus to secure the network.
team.contributors.bios_bio:
Designs and builds the CPUNK web presence, creating visual identity
                and user experience across project websites.
team.contributors.cc_bio:
Involved in ecosystem coordination, technical support, and helping
                new users and contributors onboard to the DNA Protocol.
team.contributors.cellgainz_bio: GUI and application design for the CPUNK ecosystem.
team.contributors.chip_bio:
AI assistant focused on social media content, community engagement,
                and digital communication for the CPUNK ecosystem.
team.contributors.desc:
A non-exhaustive list of community members who contribute to
              development, infrastructure, communication, and ecosystem growth.
team.contributors.label: Contributors
team.contributors.nocdem_bio:
Lead designer of DNA Nodus, DNA Connect, and DNAC.
                Architects the core protocols and infrastructure powering the CPUNK ecosystem.
team.contributors.one_bio:
Manages CPUNK social media presence across Instagram, Twitter,
                TikTok, and other platforms.
team.contributors.title: People helping CPUNK move forward
team.contributors.trader_bio:
Focused on community engagement, education, and awareness around
                CPUNK and post-quantum infrastructure.
team.contributors.punk_bio:
AI assistant focused on development, code review, documentation,
                and technical tooling for the CPUNK ecosystem.
team.cta.text:
Download DNA Connect, join the conversation, and find a piece of the project
              you want to improve. Every meaningful contributor once started as a community member.
team.cta.title: Join the movement
team.downloads.desc: Encrypted messaging, multi-chain wallet, and decentralized identity — one app, three platforms.
team.downloads.label: Downloads
team.downloads.title: Get DNA Connect
team.hero.eyebrow_text: Built by the people who use it
team.hero.meta_no_vc: No VC unlocks · No corporate owner
team.hero.panel_body_html:
The project depends on a mix ofcode, infrastructure,
                  writing, design, research, and community work.
team.hero.pill_text: No permission needed to start contributing
team.hero.subtitle_html:
CPUNK is anopen, community-driven protocolfor
                post-quantum identity and messaging. No central company, no closed team —
                anyone with passion for privacy and the post-quantum future can contribute.
team.hero.title: Built by the Community
token.desc: $CPUNK powers the entire ecosystem — from DAO governance and on-chain voting to DNA identity registration and in-app transfers. Integrated natively into DNA Connect, it's the fuel behind every action in the network. Fair-launched, hard-capped, and owned by the community. No insiders, no gatekeepers — just a movement you can be part of.
token.fairlaunch.desc: 10 public DEX orders over 5 weeks. No private rounds, no seed investors. Equal access for everyone.
token.fairlaunch.title: Fair Launch
token.label: The Token
token.quantum.desc: CF20 token on Cellframe — NIST Category 5 post-quantum cryptography. ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87, SHA-3-512.
token.quantum.title: Quantum Safe
token.supply.desc: Fixed supply. No minting, no hidden unlocks, no inflation. What exists is all there will ever be.
token.title: One token. Real utility. Community owned.
token.zeroinflation.desc: No emission schedule, no unlocks, no dilution. Your share stays your share — forever.
token.zeroinflation.title: Zero Inflation
wallet.chains.cellframeDesc: Post-quantum blockchain withDilithium signatures(NIST Category 5). Native CPUNK token and CF20 standard. Wallet files use the.dwalletformat.
wallet.chains.desc: Each chain is a first-class citizen with native transaction building, token support, and RPC integration. No wrapped tokens, no bridges — real on-chain transactions.
wallet.chains.ethereumDesc: Full Ethereum mainnet support withERC-20 token transfers, RLP encoding, and dynamic gas estimation with slow/normal/fast presets.
wallet.chains.label: Chains
wallet.chains.solanaDesc: High-throughput chain withEd25519 signaturesand SPL token standard. SLIP-10 derivation ensures compatibility with the Solana ecosystem.
wallet.chains.title: Supported blockchains
wallet.chains.tronDesc: TRON mainnet withTRC-20 token support. Uses secp256k1 signing with Base58Check address encoding. Full RPC integration for balance queries and transfers.
wallet.cta.text:
Download DNA Messenger, join the conversation, and find a piece of the project
              you want to improve. Every meaningful contributor once started as a community member.
wallet.cta.title: Join the CPUNK movement
wallet.derivation.desc1: Each blockchain uses its own derivation method to produce keys from the same BIP-39 seed material. The diagram shows the full flow from mnemonic to chain-specific wallets.
wallet.derivation.desc2: When new blockchain support is added,missing wallets are created automaticallyon identity load — no user action required.
wallet.derivation.heading: One Mnemonic, Four Derivation Paths
wallet.derivation.label: Architecture
wallet.derivation.title: How key derivation works
wallet.downloads.desc: Encrypted messaging, multi-chain wallet, and decentralized identity — one app, three platforms.
wallet.downloads.label: Downloads
wallet.downloads.title: Get DNA Connect
wallet.hero.seedLabel: All wallets derived fromone BIP-39 seed phrase
wallet.hero.subtitle:
DNA-Wallet is themulti-chain wallet built into DNA-Messenger.
                A single BIP-39 seed phrase derives wallets acrossfour blockchains—
                Cellframe, Ethereum, Solana, and TRON. Send and receive tokens without leaving the chat.
wallet.hero.title: DNA-WalletOne Seed, Every Chain
wallet.overview.desc: DNA-Wallet is not a separate app. It lives inside DNA-Messenger — every conversation is also a payment channel. No address copying, no app switching, no friction.
wallet.overview.inChatDesc: Send tokens to anyDNA contact directly inside a conversation. The recipient's wallet address is resolved automatically from their DNA identity — no raw addresses needed.
wallet.overview.label: Overview
wallet.overview.multiChainDesc: One wallet interface, four blockchains managed from a single unified view. Each chain uses its native signing algorithm — seesupported chainsfor details.
wallet.overview.oneSeedDesc: A singleBIP-39 mnemonicis the master key. Back up one seed phrase — recover all your wallets. New chains are added automatically with no re-backup needed.
wallet.overview.title: Multi-chain, built into the messenger
wallet.security.desc: Your master seed is encrypted at rest usingKyber1024— a NIST-standardized post-quantum key encapsulation mechanism. Even if your device storage is compromised, the seed remains protected against both classical and quantum attacks.
wallet.security.diagramOnDemandNote: Private keys never persist in memory or on disk longer than needed
wallet.security.heading: Kyber1024Encrypted Seed Storage
wallet.security.label: Security
wallet.security.li1: Encrypted storage:Kyber1024 private key (3,168 bytes) — quantum-safe at rest
wallet.security.li2: Secure memory:Private keys wiped after signing viasecure_memzero()
wallet.security.li3: On-demand derivation:Keys derived, used, and cleared without ever touching disk
wallet.security.li4: Local only:All signing happens on your device — private keys never leave the client
wallet.security.title: Post-quantum seed protection
